violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. In the walk in cooler for the meat department raw bacon was stored over cheese curds and sausages. Packaged steaks was stored over containers of ready to eat foods.

Demonstration of conformance with variance. — --License holder did not demonstrate conformance with approved variance or HACCP procedures. Cooler logs for vacuum sealed deli meats was not being maintained according to the HACCP plan. Dates for these logs must also contain the year the temp was taken.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Flats of turkey sandwiches in the front sandwich display case were not marked with a date they were made, or a date to sell by.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. Containers of potato salad on the top shelf of the meat display case were dated sell by August 4th. These need to be discarded.

Demonstration of conformance with variance. — --License holder did not demonstrate conformance with approved variance or HACCP procedures. Temperature logs for the coolers were not being maintained as required for the HACCP plan for sliced vacuum sealed deli meats. These must be maintained.
Approved, safe, unadulterated, approved additives, proper receiving temperature (4 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
